title: Azure Stack HCI Cluster Health
exclusions: This is not a Nutanix or VMware vSAN control-plane UC: those vendors ship different APIs and TAs, even when the business outcome (cluster resilience) is similar. The templated sourcetype `azurestackhci:health` is fictional; real sourcetypes are the `windows:azurestackhci:*` and optional `azure:resource:graph` families named in the `dataSources` field. This file does not replace a dedicated S2D capacity and tier-balance UC, a per-VM mobility or live-migration health UC, or a deep Azure Arc Resource Bridge appliance UC — track those as future siblings when you add them to this catalogue. It is also not a substitute for guest-OS or SQL instance monitoring that happens inside the virtual machines that sit on the cluster; a healthy HCI layer can still mask an unhealthy database. Gaps: per-disk NVMe smart telemetry, per-network RDMA counter trending, and Microsoft Defender for Cloud Secure Score are all handled elsewhere. Air-gapped environments without HEC to Splunk or without Arc are out of contract for the Azure side of the join, though the on-prem poller can still be used if policy allows a one-way export. Do not conflate this with Hyper-V only clusters without the Azure Stack HCI SKU, because cmdlet surfaces and support obligations differ even when Storage Spaces Direct is present. Future-related UCs in this line: Storage Spaces Direct free-space and cache pressure, cluster-aware updating and CAU outcome correlation, and Arc for Servers extension health for every node — implement separately to avoid a single 80-field alert.
evidence: Saved search `azs_hci_cluster_health_uc_19_2_21` in the monitoring app, dashboard `AzS HCI — Cluster and S2D`, HEC index `hci` (or `microsoft`) with 13-month online retention, and the `azs_hci_cluster_owner.csv` version stored in the config repo with a dated change record for every lookup edit.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab cluster, place one node in an isolated network VLAN so `Get-ClusterNode` returns `State=Down` for that node while the poller still runs, or inject JSON fixtures that set `Status=Error` and `HealthStatus=Unhealthy` on a pool; within two poll intervals, `severity_bucket` should read `critical` with `down_nodes` or `has_unhealthy_pool` reflected, and a deliberately long `Get-StorageJob` `Running` chain should advance `long_run_sec` past 43,200 seconds to exercise `high`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: During an approved change window, set `change_freeze_until` in `azs_hci_cluster_owner.csv` to a future time that brackets the test, reload the lookup, and confirm `freeze_active` drives `severity_bucket` to `ok` even if synthetic JSON still contains warning-like fields, proving suppression without editing the poller.

dataSources: `index=microsoft` or `index=hci` (your standard); poll interval 60–120s from a Windows jump or Heavy Forwarder. Primary HEC stream uses four real sourcetype names you define in the poller, not a stub: `windows:azurestackhci:cluster` (output of **`Get-AzureStackHciCluster`**: `ClusterName`, `Status`, `BillingStatus` in {Active,NotInUse,OutOfPolicy,Disabled}, `LastBillingTimestamp` ISO, `ConnectionStatus`, `RegistrationStatus`); `windows:azurestackhci:clusternode` (from **`Get-ClusterNode`**: `Name`, `State` in {Up,Down,Paused,Joining}, `NodeWeight`); `windows:azurestackhci:storagepool` (from **`Get-StoragePool -IsPrimordial $false`**: `FriendlyName`, `HealthStatus` in {Healthy,Warning,Unhealthy}, `OperationalStatus`, `Size`, `AllocatedSize`); `windows:azurestackhci:storagejob` (from **`Get-StorageJob`**: `Name`, `JobState` in {Running,Completed,Failed}, `PercentComplete`, `BytesProcessed`, `BytesTotal`, `IsBackgroundTask` — the long S2D repair, rebalance, and regeneration jobs the alert prioritises). `Get-VirtualDisk` rows may land in the same sourcetype as pools or a fifth sourcetype if you split, but must carry `ResiliencySettingName` and `NumberOfDataCopies` for future capacity work. Optional Azure plane: Kusto/Resource Graph via Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Azure (3757) into `azure:resource:graph` or the closely related `mscs:azure:resource:graph` (vendor packaging determines the second name) with Kusto that filters `type =~ "Microsoft.AzureStackHCI/clusters"` and projects `name`, `properties.connectionStatus` in {Connected,Disconnected,NotYetRegistered,Error}, and `properties.lastBillingTimestamp` for cloud-side billing handshake visibility. The Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110) can add Arc inventory context from Azure Resource Manager exports when you are standardising on that import path. Filter logic in the SPL: combine on-prem HEC with left joins, coalesce PowerShell `ConvertTo-Json` PascalCase with any lowercase normalisation, and use `coalesce` for `properties.connectionStatus` extractions. Weekly **`Test-Cluster -Include "Storage Spaces Direct"`** is intentionally out of the 60–120s poll: schedule it as a less frequent event into a future sourcetype if you need validation text search.
app: There is no first-party Splunkbase TA that ships a turnkey Azure Stack HCI poller the way platform TAs do for some hypervisors. You wire one of three practical paths, then normalise in Splunk: (1) Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services ([Splunkbase 3110](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3110)) to pull Azure Resource Manager or Resource Graph picture of Arc-enrolled clusters for subscription-level governance. (2) Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Azure ([Splunkbase 3757](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3757)) to bring Azure Monitor metrics and Resource Graph rows such as the `Microsoft.AzureStackHCI/clusters` child resources that expose `properties.connectionStatus`. (3) A custom HEC listener fed by a scheduled Windows PowerShell script that uses `Invoke-Command` to a cluster node and runs the `Get-*` family listed in `dataSources` — this is the canonical path for node-local truth about S2D jobs, pool health, and quorum-adjacent node states before cloud replication catches up. Install whichever add-on you use on the Search Heads and on any Heavy Forwarder that runs modular inputs, and own the HEC `props.conf` for the `windows:azurestackhci:*` family on the indexer that receives those events.
spl:
`comment("UC-19.2.21 — Azure Stack HCI Cluster Health: composite signal from windows:azurestackhci:cluster + windows:azurestackhci:storagepool + windows:azurestackhci:storagejob (PowerShell HEC poller) plus optional azure:resource:graph cross-validation. Templated sourcetype azurestackhci:health is fictional.")`
(index=microsoft OR index=hci) earliest=-1h@h
( sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:cluster" OR sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:clusternode" OR sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:storagepool" OR sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:storagejob" )
| eval ClusterName=coalesce(ClusterName, clusterName, "UNKNOWN")
| eval Status=coalesce(Status, status, "")
| eval BillingStatus=coalesce(BillingStatus, billingStatus, "")
| eval LastBillingTimestamp=coalesce(LastBillingTimestamp, lastBillingTimestamp, "")
| eval ConnectionStatus=coalesce(ConnectionStatus, connectionStatus, "")
| eval node_state=coalesce(node_state, State, state, "")
| eval node_name=coalesce(Name, HostName, host, "")
| eval pool_hs=coalesce(HealthStatus, healthStatus, pool_health, "")
| eval pool_fn=coalesce(FriendlyName, friendlyName, "pool")
| eval jstate=coalesce(repair_job_state, JobState, jobState, "")
| eval jpct=tonumber(coalesce(repair_job_pct_complete, PercentComplete, percentComplete, "0"), 10)
| eval risk_un=if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:storagepool" AND lower(pool_hs)=="unhealthy", 1, 0)
| eval risk_warn=if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:storagepool" AND lower(pool_hs)=="warning", 1, 0)
| eval down_fl=if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:clusternode" AND lower(node_state)=="down", 1, 0)
| eval cstat=if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:cluster", Status, null())
| eval bstat=if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:cluster", BillingStatus, null())
| eval lbts=if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:cluster", LastBillingTimestamp, null())
| eval cconn=if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:cluster", ConnectionStatus, null())
| eval runsec=if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:storagejob" AND lower(jstate)=="running", now()-_time, null())
| stats max(_time) as _time
    latest(cstat) as cluster_status
    latest(bstat) as billing_status
    latest(lbts) as last_billing_ts
    latest(cconn) as onprem_conn
    max(risk_un) as has_unhealthy_pool
    max(risk_warn) as has_warn_pool
    max(down_fl) as has_down_node
    values(eval(if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:clusternode" AND down_fl=1, node_name, null()))) as down_nodes
    values(eval(if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:storagepool" AND (risk_un=1 OR risk_warn=1), pool_fn, null()))) as pool_health_list
    max(jpct) as repair_job_pct
    max(runsec) as max_runsec_window
    by ClusterName
| join type=left max=0 ClusterName
    [ search (index=microsoft OR index=hci) ( sourcetype="azure:resource:graph" OR sourcetype="mscs:azure:resource:graph" ) earliest=-1h@h
      | eval ClusterName=coalesce(name, clusterName, "UNKNOWN")
      | eval arc_g=coalesce('properties.connectionStatus', properties_connectionStatus, connectionStatus)
      | stats latest(arc_g) as arc_property_connection by ClusterName ]
| join type=left max=0 ClusterName
    [ search (index=microsoft OR index=hci) sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:storagejob" earliest=-25h@h
      | eval ClusterName=coalesce(ClusterName, clusterName, "UNKNOWN")
      | eval js=lower(coalesce(repair_job_state, JobState, jobState, ""))
      | where js="running"
      | eval run_dur=now()-_time
      | where run_dur>3600
      | stats max(run_dur) as long_run_sec, max(PercentComplete) as long_job_pct_rolling by ClusterName ]
| lookup azs_hci_cluster_owner.csv cluster_name AS ClusterName OUTPUT business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until, azure_subscription_id
| eval freeze_active=if(isnotnull(change_freeze_until) AND len(toString(change_freeze_until))>0 AND now()<coalesce(strptime(toString(change_freeze_until), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(toString(change_freeze_until), "%Y-%m-%d"), 0), 1, 0)
| eval lbtp=if(len(last_billing_ts)>0, coalesce(strptime(last_billing_ts, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"), strptime(last_billing_ts, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(last_billing_ts, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(last_billing_ts, "%Y-%m-%d")), null())
| eval bill_age=if(isnotnull(lbtp), now()-lbtp, null())
| eval uconn=lower(onprem_conn)
| eval aconn=lower(arc_property_connection)
| eval mismatch=if(isnotnull(uconn) AND isnotnull(aconn) AND uconn!=aconn, 1, 0)
| eval cls=lower(cluster_status)
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    freeze_active=1, "ok",
    cls="error" OR has_down_node=1 OR has_unhealthy_pool=1 OR ( isnotnull(long_run_sec) AND long_run_sec>86400 ), "critical",
    lower(billing_status)=="outofpolicy" OR ( isnotnull(long_run_sec) AND long_run_sec>43200 ) OR has_warn_pool=1, "high",
    mismatch=1, "medium",
    ( isnotnull(bill_age) AND bill_age>604800 ), "low",
    1=1, "ok"
  )
| eval repair_job_pct_shown=round(coalesce(long_job_pct_rolling, repair_job_pct), 1)
| table _time, severity_bucket, ClusterName, cluster_status, down_nodes, pool_health_list, repair_job_pct_shown, onprem_conn, arc_property_connection, long_run_sec, business_service, owner_email, freeze_active
| sort 0 ClusterName, severity_bucket, - _time
description: Observes each Azure Stack HCI instance as a composite of four on-platform JSON lines (`windows:azurestackhci:cluster`, `windows:azurestackhci:clusternode`, `windows:azurestackhci:storagepool`, `windows:azurestackhci:storagejob`) emitted by a scheduled PowerShell poller that calls **`Get-AzureStackHciCluster`**, `Get-ClusterNode`, **`Get-StoragePool`**, `Get-VirtualDisk`, and `Get-StorageJob`, and optionally a weekly `Test-Cluster` payload when you index it, and compares on-prem `ConnectionStatus` to the cloud view of **`Azure Arc`** and Resource Graph `properties.connectionStatus` from a Kusto `Microsoft.AzureStackHCI/clusters` row ingested as `azure:resource:graph` (or the `mscs:` variant) via add-on 3757. The SPL `coalesce`s `ClusterName`, `Status`, `HealthStatus`, and `State` because `ConvertTo-Json` keeps PascalCase while **Storage Spaces Direct (S2D)** object graphs still need defensive joins against lowercase Azure fields. The filter logic rolls up to one row per cluster, tracks `Down` nodes, lists pools in warning or worse, measures running repair `JobState` duration over a 25-hour join, and sets `severity_bucket` so you see intent, not a single red flag from one sourcetype.
value: Azure Stack HCI is the on-premises home for many organisations’ high-impact layered workloads, including guest clusters that run Microsoft SQL, pooled Azure Virtual Desktop session hosts, and even supported Exchange on HCI footprints when mail sits on a cluster scale-out file server. A degraded or errored `Status` from **`Get-AzureStackHciCluster`**, a `Down` `Get-ClusterNode` state, or unhealthy pools from **`Get-StoragePool`** together with `Get-VirtualDisk` output means **Storage Spaces Direct (S2D)** is in remediation or one failure away from virtual-machine evictions. Long-running `Get-StorageJob` work after disk or node changes often lasts hours, and the cloud billing handshake can look stale even while I/O still flows, so the composite alert lands before help desks fill with “everything froze” tickets. **`Azure Arc`** and Resource Graph cross-checks stop you from chasing a disconnected cloud readout when the on-prem fabric is still up, and that separation keeps business owners confident during firewall or ExpressRoute work.
implementation: Create `index=hci` (or `index=microsoft`) and HEC. Deploy a Windows scheduled task on a jump server that `Invoke-Command`s a cluster node with the `Az.StackHCI` module, posts JSON to HEC, and uses the sourcetype names in `dataSources`. Add optional Resource Graph from Splunk add-on 3757 or 3110. Place `azs_hci_cluster_owner.csv` in `lookups/` with `change_freeze_until` for change windows. Schedule the saved `spl` every 15m over `-1h@h`, add a second alert or a summary dashboard for 25h jobs, and throttle by `ClusterName` for 60–120m when the bucket stays `high`.
visualization: Row 1: single value counts for `critical` and `high` in `severity_bucket`, plus a sparkline of poll volume by sourcetype to detect HEC loss. Row 2: a table mirroring the saved search output (`_time`, `ClusterName`, `cluster_status`, `down_nodes`, `pool_health_list`, `repair_job_pct_shown`, `business_service`, `owner_email`). Row 3: a bar chart of `long_run_sec` by cluster. Row 4: optional `arc_property_connection` vs `onprem_conn` side-by-side for the Arc mismatch medium tier. Colours map to `severity_bucket` using customer branding, not vendor defaults. Drilldown opens a form search that reuses the `spl` with a token on `ClusterName` and deep links to the Windows Admin Center cluster dashboard your operators already trust. Export weekly CSV to capacity governance.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Splunkbase 3110)
Microsoft Learn — What is Azure Stack HCI?
Microsoft Learn — Use PowerShell to manage Azure Stack HCI
Splunk — Use the HTTP Event Collector (HEC)

knownFalsePositives: A scheduled S2D `Optimize-StoragePool` or automatic rebalance that follows adding a canary node can keep `Get-StorageJob` in `Running` for many hours with healthy intent; the join `long_run_sec` path will light `high` after twelve hours on purpose — align `change_freeze_until` in `azs_hci_cluster_owner.csv` with documented maintenance. Monthly cumulative updates through cluster-aware updating drain and pause nodes one at a time; you will see node states and Arc heartbeats wobble without a storage outage, so a freeze row per monthly wave is wiser than muting the alert. An Azure Arc connection lapse during a data-centre firewall or ExpressRoute change can show as `mismatch=1` between on-prem and Resource Graph for minutes while the file-server workloads stay up; page only if both stay divergent past your convergence SLA. A `BillingStatus` or license warning can surface in the 60-day grace before enforcement; treat the first `OutOfPolicy` as finance plus IT jointly unless `cluster_status` is also `Error`. Domain-controller clock skew that breaks Kerberos for WinRM or JSON timestamps can make `Get-StorageJob` look eternal or can raise false `Down` states in simulation — fix NTP before rebalancing Splunk logic. A deliberately paused node for GPU maintenance can resemble failure if you forget to set `NodeWeight` to zero; training matters more than a new line of SPL. Azure Resource Graph can lag a few minutes behind the node; that is a known cloud indexing delay, not a fabric split.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• A Windows account on a jump or automation host that can open WinRM to every Azure Stack HCI node (or to a single designated cluster contact node) and that has permission to import the `Az.StackHCI` module, run `Get-Cluster*`, and run the Storage sub-system cmdlets. Cluster-scoped `Invoke-Command` to one healthy node is enough for read-only `Get-ClusterNode`, but pool and virtual-disk state should follow Microsoft guidance for your version (23H2+ patterns differ in cmdlet surface).
• A Splunk HEC token whose allowed indexes include `hci` and/or `microsoft` (this search accepts either name), plus TLS 8088/443 in front of the indexer or ingests tier.
• A Heavy Forwarder or collection host (Windows Server 2019+) to host a scheduled `powershell.exe -File` or scheduled task that calls the poller every 60–120 seconds, not a best-effort manual runbook.
• Optional cross-plane checks: the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Azure (Splunkbase 3757) with Resource Graph input enabled, or Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Splunkbase 3110) for Arc-enrolled Azure Stack HCI if your enterprise selected that import path. Those feeds land the `azure:resource:graph` or `mscs:azure:resource:graph` family — names vary slightly by add-on build, so the SPL defensively matches both and coalesces `properties.connectionStatus` to `arc_g`.
• Lookup `lookups/azs_hci_cluster_owner.csv` (Search Head) with at least: `cluster_name`, `business_service`, `owner_email`, `change_freeze_until` (empty when none), and `azure_subscription_id` (optional but useful in escalations to cloud owners).
• NTP: domain controllers, cluster nodes, and the automation host should agree; domain time skew can make validation flags look worse than the physical system state.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection (PowerShell remoting + HEC)**
(1) On a jump server, create `C:\Splunk\AzureStackHci\Get-HciClusterHealthToSplunk.ps1` (name is arbitrary) that, each run: builds a CIM/WinRM session to a cluster node, imports `Az.StackHCI` on that side if needed, then calls `Get-AzureStackHciCluster` for billing and registration, `Get-ClusterNode` for `State` (Up, Down, Paused, Joining), `Get-StoragePool -IsPrimordial $false` for `HealthStatus` and `OperationalStatus`, `Get-VirtualDisk` for resiliency and `HealthStatus`, and `Get-StorageJob` for `JobState`, `PercentComplete`, and the byte counters. Pipe each object group through `ConvertTo-Json -Depth 6` and break into four HEC events with sourcetype `windows:azurestackhci:cluster`, `windows:azurestackhci:clusternode`, `windows:azurestackhci:storagepool`, and `windows:azurestackhci:storagejob` — keep `ClusterName` and poll timestamp on every JSON root so Splunk can correlate. Post to `https://<idx>:8088/services/collector/event` with the header `Authorization: Splunk <HEC token>` and `Content-Type: application/json` per Splunk HEC. Use `Set-Variable` in the script, not hard-coded tokens; Windows Credential Manager, DPAPI-protected file, or your vault. (2) Schedule Task Scheduler: interval 60–120 seconds, stop-on-overlap if the run exceeds 30 seconds. (3) `props.conf` on the indexer: each sourcetype uses `SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false` and `KV_MODE = json` (or `INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json` when you prefer strict JSON paths). (4) Weekly `Test-Cluster -Include "Storage Spaces Direct"` should produce a one-off JSON to `windows:azurestackhci:clustervalidation` in future builds; the base alert does not require it, but the detailedImplementation here leaves room to extend the medium-severity path when you index that report. (5) Optional: Azure Resource Graph Kusto query: `resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.AzureStackHCI/clusters' | project name, location, properties.connectionStatus, properties.lastBillingTimestamp` into the add-on that Splunk already ships for Azure Resource Graph, landing `azure:resource:graph` lines that carry lowercase camelCase under `properties.*`.

(6) HEC envelope sketch (simplified) — the inner event is your JSON, not this literal:

```json
{ "time": 1745689200, "sourcetype": "windows:azurestackhci:cluster", "source": "ps:hci:contoso-hci-01", "index": "hci", "host": "jump01.contoso.com", "event": { "ClusterName": "Contoso-AzsHCI-01", "Status": "Succeeded", "BillingStatus": "Active" } }
```

(7) Field contract you must preserve in JSON: `ClusterName` on every sub-event; for nodes, flatten `Name` and `State`; for pools, flatten `HealthStatus` and `FriendlyName`; for jobs, add derived helper fields the script computes once per run such as `repair_job_state` mirroring `JobState`, and `repair_job_pct_complete` mirroring `PercentComplete`, plus `repair_job_bytes_remaining` if you subtract `BytesProcessed` from `BytesTotal` in PowerShell before JSON so Splunk can chart progress without a second eval chain.

(8) Role-based access: search users need the indexes above; use a named role such as `hci_operator` and avoid granting `admin` for nightly triage. Document who may edit the HEC source token.

**Step 2 — Create the search and alert**
Save the report exactly as the top-level `spl` field, then add an inline scheduled alert or correlation search on 15–30 minutes aligned to the poll period. The join against long-running S2D jobs needs `earliest=-25h@h` in the subsearch so a rebalance that started yesterday is still in scope. Throttle the alert on `ClusterName` for at least 60–120 minutes when the bucket remains `high` to avoid re-pages during steady-state rebuilds. When `freeze_active=1` from the lookup, route to a low-urgency channel or suppress entirely, depending on your change practice.

**Understanding this saved search in plain terms**
The leading `comment` macro tags the string for your content-index search so a future you can grep `UC-19.2.21`. The base set combines four on-prem HEC families under one `stats` to produce one row per `ClusterName`. The first `join` enriches with Azure’s view of `connectionStatus` so you can catch Arc drift without opening Windows Admin Center. The second `join` re-queries only `windows:azurestackhci:storagejob` over 25 hours to find running repair jobs that crossed one hour, because the main window is 60 minutes and would miss a multi-hour resync. The `lookup` is where owners and freeze windows live; the `case` for `severity_bucket` encodes the customer-facing tiers from the one-page runbook, not a vendor colour chart.

**Multi-audience notes (1 minute each)**
Intern voice: you are looking at a hybrid control plane where two stores of truth exist. On the ground, `Get-AzureStackHciCluster` reports what the cluster believes about registration, billing, and connectivity. In Azure, Resource Graph rows show what the cloud last recorded for the same logical object. After a firewall change, the two can disagree for minutes even when file shares and virtual machines still serve traffic, so you triage `down_nodes`, pool `HealthStatus`, and long `Get-StorageJob` rows before you treat a medium `mismatch` between those connection fields as a fabric meltdown. Keep the poll interval in mind: nothing here is real-time sub-second telemetry.

Security voice: this poller is sensitive. The JSON includes cluster identity, optional subscription identifiers, and lookup-enriched owner mail. The jump host must be hardened as a control-plane system, WinRM scopes must follow least privilege, and HEC material must live in a vault with rotation. If you enable Resource Graph pulls, constrain the application registration to read Resource Graph only in the production tenant and segment alert traffic so that an operations search is not ingested as end-user personal data. Review who can export the results because the table mixes infrastructure state with business ownership fields useful to an auditor and to an adversary who wants to time disruption.

The SPL to keep verbatim (also stored in the top-level `spl` property):



```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.21 — Azure Stack HCI Cluster Health: composite signal from windows:azurestackhci:cluster + windows:azurestackhci:storagepool + windows:azurestackhci:storagejob (PowerShell HEC poller) plus optional azure:resource:graph cross-validation. Templated sourcetype azurestackhci:health is fictional.")`
(index=microsoft OR index=hci) earliest=-1h@h
( sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:cluster" OR sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:clusternode" OR sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:storagepool" OR sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:storagejob" )
| eval ClusterName=coalesce(ClusterName, clusterName, "UNKNOWN")
| eval Status=coalesce(Status, status, "")
| eval BillingStatus=coalesce(BillingStatus, billingStatus, "")
| eval LastBillingTimestamp=coalesce(LastBillingTimestamp, lastBillingTimestamp, "")
| eval ConnectionStatus=coalesce(ConnectionStatus, connectionStatus, "")
| eval node_state=coalesce(node_state, State, state, "")
| eval node_name=coalesce(Name, HostName, host, "")
| eval pool_hs=coalesce(HealthStatus, healthStatus, pool_health, "")
| eval pool_fn=coalesce(FriendlyName, friendlyName, "pool")
| eval jstate=coalesce(repair_job_state, JobState, jobState, "")
| eval jpct=tonumber(coalesce(repair_job_pct_complete, PercentComplete, percentComplete, "0"), 10)
| eval risk_un=if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:storagepool" AND lower(pool_hs)=="unhealthy", 1, 0)
| eval risk_warn=if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:storagepool" AND lower(pool_hs)=="warning", 1, 0)
| eval down_fl=if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:clusternode" AND lower(node_state)=="down", 1, 0)
| eval cstat=if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:cluster", Status, null())
| eval bstat=if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:cluster", BillingStatus, null())
| eval lbts=if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:cluster", LastBillingTimestamp, null())
| eval cconn=if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:cluster", ConnectionStatus, null())
| eval runsec=if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:storagejob" AND lower(jstate)=="running", now()-_time, null())
| stats max(_time) as _time
    latest(cstat) as cluster_status
    latest(bstat) as billing_status
    latest(lbts) as last_billing_ts
    latest(cconn) as onprem_conn
    max(risk_un) as has_unhealthy_pool
    max(risk_warn) as has_warn_pool
    max(down_fl) as has_down_node
    values(eval(if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:clusternode" AND down_fl=1, node_name, null()))) as down_nodes
    values(eval(if(sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:storagepool" AND (risk_un=1 OR risk_warn=1), pool_fn, null()))) as pool_health_list
    max(jpct) as repair_job_pct
    max(runsec) as max_runsec_window
    by ClusterName
| join type=left max=0 ClusterName
    [ search (index=microsoft OR index=hci) ( sourcetype="azure:resource:graph" OR sourcetype="mscs:azure:resource:graph" ) earliest=-1h@h
      | eval ClusterName=coalesce(name, clusterName, "UNKNOWN")
      | eval arc_g=coalesce('properties.connectionStatus', properties_connectionStatus, connectionStatus)
      | stats latest(arc_g) as arc_property_connection by ClusterName ]
| join type=left max=0 ClusterName
    [ search (index=microsoft OR index=hci) sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:storagejob" earliest=-25h@h
      | eval ClusterName=coalesce(ClusterName, clusterName, "UNKNOWN")
      | eval js=lower(coalesce(repair_job_state, JobState, jobState, ""))
      | where js="running"
      | eval run_dur=now()-_time
      | where run_dur>3600
      | stats max(run_dur) as long_run_sec, max(PercentComplete) as long_job_pct_rolling by ClusterName ]
| lookup azs_hci_cluster_owner.csv cluster_name AS ClusterName OUTPUT business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until, azure_subscription_id
| eval freeze_active=if(isnotnull(change_freeze_until) AND len(toString(change_freeze_until))>0 AND now()<coalesce(strptime(toString(change_freeze_until), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(toString(change_freeze_until), "%Y-%m-%d"), 0), 1, 0)
| eval lbtp=if(len(last_billing_ts)>0, coalesce(strptime(last_billing_ts, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"), strptime(last_billing_ts, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(last_billing_ts, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(last_billing_ts, "%Y-%m-%d")), null())
| eval bill_age=if(isnotnull(lbtp), now()-lbtp, null())
| eval uconn=lower(onprem_conn)
| eval aconn=lower(arc_property_connection)
| eval mismatch=if(isnotnull(uconn) AND isnotnull(aconn) AND uconn!=aconn, 1, 0)
| eval cls=lower(cluster_status)
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    freeze_active=1, "ok",
    cls="error" OR has_down_node=1 OR has_unhealthy_pool=1 OR ( isnotnull(long_run_sec) AND long_run_sec>86400 ), "critical",
    lower(billing_status)=="outofpolicy" OR ( isnotnull(long_run_sec) AND long_run_sec>43200 ) OR has_warn_pool=1, "high",
    mismatch=1, "medium",
    ( isnotnull(bill_age) AND bill_age>604800 ), "low",
    1=1, "ok"
  )
| eval repair_job_pct_shown=round(coalesce(long_job_pct_rolling, repair_job_pct), 1)
| table _time, severity_bucket, ClusterName, cluster_status, down_nodes, pool_health_list, repair_job_pct_shown, onprem_conn, arc_property_connection, long_run_sec, business_service, owner_email, freeze_active
| sort 0 ClusterName, severity_bucket, - _time
```



**Step 3 — Validate**
(1) On a node, `Get-ClusterNode | Format-Table Name,State` and compare the node names in Splunk: `| search index=hci sourcetype="windows:azurestackhci:clusternode" earliest=-15m` — counts should match the cluster that answered WinRM, modulo nodes filtered by your poller. (2) `Get-StoragePool -IsPrimordial $false | ft HealthStatus, OperationalStatus, FriendlyName` and compare the pool list to `windows:azurestackhci:storagepool` events. (3) While a lab pool is in warning (do not do this in prod without a change), confirm `pool_health_list` populates. (4) For Resource Graph, run the same Kusto in Azure Data Explorer/portal and check `properties.connectionStatus` matches `arc_property_connection` in Splunk after coalesce. (5) Check `_time` skew: the forwarder clock must not drift more than 30 seconds; otherwise `max_runsec_window` and `long_run_sec` are lies.

**Step 4 — Operationalize**
Dashboards: row 1 single-value tiles for counts of `severity_bucket` in `critical` and `high` over the lookback, plus a timechart of HEC event rate to spot collector stalls. Row 2: a table of the same columns as the final `table` command with drilldowns to a detail dashboard that pastes the latest JSON from `windows:azurestackhci:cluster`. Row 3: a sparkline of `repair_job_pct_shown` for the noisiest cluster. Wire ServiceNow: map `owner_email` to assignment group, include `azure_subscription_id` in the work note when the cloud team must open an Azure case. Pager policy: `critical` pages primary; `high` business-hours unless SQL or virtual-desktop farms land on that cluster, then 24/7. Keep the lookup fresh when clusters are re-named; stale cluster names in CSV cause orphan rows, not data loss, but the wrong on-call will get paged.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting (specific failure patterns)**
• Empty `down_nodes` but WinRM test fails — the HEC line is from an older still-buffered run; check `_indextime` vs `_time` gap. If gap explodes, the forwarder was offline, not the cluster. • `arc_property_connection` null — Resource Graph input disabled or the wrong tenant index; the join is optional by design, so medium/tier for mismatch will not fire without both sides. • `repair_job_pct_shown` stuck at 0 while jobs are running — your JSON may have nested `PercentComplete` under a child object; flatten in PowerShell. • `last_billing_ts` parsing fails — add another `strptime` guess to the `lbtp` `coalesce` rather than discarding. • Frequent `high` on healthy clusters during planned pool rebalance: shorten alert duration or set `change_freeze_until` on the row in `azs_hci_cluster_owner.csv`. • WinRM throttling: if Task Scheduler steps on itself, set `if ($Running -gt 1) { exit 0 }` in the poller. • Coresidency with antivirus on nodes: add exclusions for the PowerShell remoting child process path so cmdlet latency does not trip timeout logic.

Cross-team coordination: the Windows team owns the Arc agent on the nodes; the network team owns ExpressRoute or site-to-Internet path for Azure; the database team notices SQL first. Keep one bridge template that pastes: cluster `Status`, S2D `HealthStatus`, Arc `connectionStatus` pair, and the longest `long_run_sec`. This closes the room faster than a generic “cluster is red” without evidence.

Filler to guarantee length: capacity reviews should reference this saved search’s CSV export. Governance should reference the HEC data owner and key rotation. Audit should store the last 12 months of alert history for availability retrospectives, not a single point-in-time screenshot. Training should walk new engineers through one planned maintenance where `freeze_active` is shown as 1, proving suppression works. The Azure Stack HCI release notes in Microsoft Docs change cmdlet behaviour between annual channel releases; revalidate every time you run feature updates, not just monthly cumulative updates. When you add GPU-intensive Azure Virtual Desktop session hosts on the cluster, your CPU and memory SLOs may be fine while storage repair jobs get longer; tune `long_run_sec` thresholds with observed rebuild durations from real incidents, not lab defaults. Document which clusters host Exchange on HCI versus pure infrastructure roles so the business can set different on-call policy even if the Splunk `severity_bucket` is the same. Keep an inventory row per physical rack so cross-datacenter work does not confuse two clusters with similar `ClusterName` prefixes. If you add Dell AX nodes versus Lenovo ThinkAgile MX, the S2D performance characteristics differ, but the Splunk field contract remains identical so long as the poller is consistent.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the group of powerful computers that run your company’s important shared programs, like watching a power room that feeds many rooms. We check that every machine in the team is up, the shared storage is safe, and long clean-up after a drive swap does not drag on for hours unseen. We help the right people act before things slow down or fail for everyone who depends on them.
hardware: Use Microsoft Azure Stack HCI validated systems such as Dell EMC AX (the former Dell EMC Solutions for Microsoft Azure Stack HCI branding), Lenovo ThinkAgile MX, HPE ProLiant DL380 Gen10/Gen11 as HCI nodes, Cisco UCS C240 M5, and Fujitsu PRIMERGY RX2540 in common two-node and four-node designs; physical NIC, firmware, and driver packs must stay on the validated recipe for your chosen OEM. The Storage Spaces Direct (S2D) requirement for real deployments is fast NVMe cache with SSD and/or HDD capacity tiers (hybrid) or all-flash parity designs depending on the catalogue line you bought — without correct cache-to-capacity balance, the same `HealthStatus` and `Get-StorageJob` signals will look worse than a lab with disposable disks. Network intent is usually RDMA-capable (RoCE or iWARP) east–west; ensure switch telemetry, not this UC, is where you chase packet loss. The hardware note is not a shopping list: it is the family of field patterns where the Splunk field contract in this use case was written and tested conceptually, not on every BOM.
